item.text: I’m going to answer this strictly via shell-based Orbit graph commands and verify each crate claim from command output. First I’ll discover the exact `orbit.graph.*` query shape, then run targeted queries for direct `orbit-knowledge` dependencies.

item.aggregated_output:
Execute a tool

Usage: orbit tool run [OPTIONS] <NAME>

Arguments:
  <NAME>  Tool name

Options:
      --input <INPUT>            JSON input for the tool (use --input-file to avoid shell escaping issues with rich content)
      --root <ROOT>              Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
      --input-file <INPUT_FILE>  Path to a JSON file to use as input (bypasses shell escaping; preferred for markdown or multi-line content)
      --agent <AGENT>            Explicit agent name for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_NAME)
      --model <MODEL>            Explicit agent model for provenance attribution (overrides ORBIT_AGENT_MODEL)
      --timeout <TIMEOUT>        Execution timeout (e.g. "30s", "5000ms")
      --dry-run                  Validate without executing
      --fields <FIELDS>          Comma-separated top-level fields to keep from object output
      --full                     Return the tool's full unfiltered JSON output
      --pretty                   Pretty-print JSON output for human debugging
      --output <OUTPUT>          Output format [default: json] [possible values: json, text]
  -h, --help                     Print help

Manage and run Orbit tools, including external MCP plugins

Usage: orbit tool [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  list      List all registered tools
  show      Show detailed information about a tool
  run       Execute a tool
  add       Register an external tool or MCP plugin
  scaffold  Generate a starter external tool plugin
  remove    Remove an external tool
  enable    Enable a disabled tool
  disable   Disable a tool
  doctor    Validate tool health

Options:
      --root <ROOT>  Override the Orbit root directory (highest precedence)
  -h, --help         Print help

Examples:
  orbit tool scaffold ./plugins/hello_orbit.py --name demo.hello
  orbit tool add ./plugins/hello_orbit.py
  orbit tool show demo.hello

item.text: I found the tool runner interface; next I’m listing registered tools so we can call the graph query endpoints directly via `orbit tool run orbit.graph.*`.

error: store error: attempt to write a readonly database

item.text: I hit an environment constraint: `orbit tool show` failed with a readonly DB write attempt. I’m testing whether `orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps` can execute without the metadata write path so we can still query dependencies.
I confirmed the graph APIs are callable. Next I’m querying graph search/refs for `orbit-knowledge` in config space so we can attribute exact `Cargo.toml` files, not just crate-edge names.
I can already see candidate consumers from `orbit.graph.deps`, but I’m now cross-checking through graph reference APIs so the final list is backed by multiple tool-verified signals.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq -r '.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\")) | .key'"
/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq -r '.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\")) | .key'"
/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq -r '.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\")) | .key' | wc -l"
/bin/zsh -lc "orbit tool run orbit.graph.deps --full | jq -r '.crates | to_entries[] | select(.value | index(\"orbit-knowledge\")) | .key' | wc -l"

item.text: The dependency query resolved exactly two crate names. I’m now validating the corresponding `Cargo.toml` paths through `orbit.graph.show` so each reported path is tool-confirmed.
